title: Diabetic foot skin check
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.
examination.rationale: NG19 directly supports clinician examination of both feet for skin integrity, callus, ulceration, infection or inflammation, deformity, neuropathy, perfusion, gangrene, and Charcot features, with clinician-entered risk classification only.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.

source.title: Diabetic foot problems: prevention and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.6 — frequency, bilateral foot examination, and clinician risk stratification

referral.text: Supports documenting completed foot protection assessment, vascular or biomechanical review, clinician-selected reassessment, information already provided, and an entered follow-up plan.
referral.rationale: The foot-service section supports recording a completed specialist assessment, information already provided, clinician-selected review plan, and arranged follow-up without generating referral or an interval.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports documenting completed foot protection assessment, vascular or biomechanical review, clinician-selected reassessment, information already provided, and an entered follow-up plan.

source.title: Diabetic foot problems: prevention and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.3.10–1.3.13 — specialist assessment, reassessment, and information

sources_used.title: Diabetic foot problems: prevention and management
sources_used.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation

source_limitations: Diabetes type and control, previous ulcer or amputation, renal replacement, bilateral skin findings, pulses, neuropathy testing, footwear, ulcer dimensions, infection, Charcot features, clinician risk category, treatment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
